title: Arthur the King Opening Weekend Projections - $8-10 Million Expected
text: Mark Wahlberg's canine drama Arthur the King is expected to open with a modest $8-10 million from 3,000 North American theaters, according to the Variety article. The film tells a true story about a professional adventure racer who befriends a wounded stray dog. With a production budget of approximately $20 million, the opening represents a moderate start for the Lionsgate release. Despite being a newcomer, the film is unlikely to top the box office. Kung Fu Panda 4 is projected to maintain its lead position with $28-30 million in its second weekend, having already accumulated $62 million domestically. The movie was directed by Simon Cellan Jones and based on Mikael Lindnord's book Arthur: The Dog Who Crossed the Jungle to Find a Home. Variety's critic noted that while the film follows a predictable path, the journey itself remains engaging. Lionsgate is hoping Arthur the King performs similarly to Channing Tatum's 2022 adventure film Dog, which opened to $15 million and ultimately grossed $84 million worldwide.
